rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT GEORGE CASTLE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/12/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The initial petition did not identify the decedent's heirs and their dates of death, as required by LRSF 14.18 C.1. and D.1., nor did it set forth the proposed executor's efforts to locate heirs. This must be done now for the record before the estate can be distributed. (2) The I&A was filed on 1/10/24, before the personal representative was appointed and had any authority to act and even before she had even filed her petition for such appointment. (3) The attorney costs appear to be incorrect. (And the filing fees were $435, not $450.) Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 11/1/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CARTE REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Specially Prepared Interrogatories; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: (part 1 of 2) This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Stephen M. Murphy Before the court is Respondent Lawrence W. Carte's Motion to Compel Further Responses to Specially Prepared Interrogatories (the "Motion"). The Motion is GRANTED for the reason set forth below. A party propounding interrogatories may move for an order compelling responses if the party to whom interrogatories are directed fails to serve timely responses. (Cal. Code of Civil Proc. sec. 2030.290(b).) On May 14, 2024, Respondent Lawrence W. Carte ("Respondent") propounded Specially Prepared Interrogatories (Set One) on Petitioner Gayle Draper-Brown ("Petitioner") by mail (See Declaration of Joseph P. Corey in Support of Respondent Lawrence W. Carte's Motion to Compel Further Responses to Specially Prepared Interrogatories ["Corey Declaration"], Exh. A.). Pursuant to Cal. Code of Civil Proc. secs. 1013 and 2030.260, Petitioner's discovery responses were due no later than June 18, 2024. Petitioner served untimely discovery responses on June 19, 2024, by mail (Corey Declaration, Exh. B.). As such, pursuant to Cal. Code of Civil Proc. sec. 2030.290(a), Petitioner's objections are waived for failure to timely serve responses. Respondent is seeking further responses to Specially Prepared Interrogatories (Set One) Nos. 2-10, 14-19, 28-30, 32 and 33 because Petitioner interposed objections in her responses and failed to substantively respond. Although Petitioner contends that she has since supplemented her discovery responses to provide substantive responses without objections (See Response to Respondent Lawrence W. Carte's Motion to Compel Further Responses to Specially Prepared Interrogatories ("Response Brief") at p. 1:25-27), Respondent disputes Petitioner's contention and asserts that Petitioner has not in fact provided supplemental responses (See Respondent's Reply to Gayle Draper-Brown's Response to Lawrence W. Carte's Motion to Compel Further Responses to Specially Prepared Interrogatories and Request for Sanctions ("Reply Brief"). Furthermore, Petitioner does not contest the instant Motion (Response Brief at p. 1:25). Petitioner Gayle Draper-Brown shall serve responses to Respondent Lawrence W. Carte's Specially Prepared Interrogatories (Set One) Nos. 2-10, 14-19, 28-30, 32 and 33, without objections, within fifteen (15) days of service of a Notice of Entry of Order. Respondent Lawrence W. Carte's request that Petitioner and her counsel be ordered to pay monetary sanctions is GRANTED pursuant to Cal. Code of Civil Proc. sec. 2023.010. A party who fails to respond or to submit to an authorized method of discovery is subject to sanctions for misuse of the discovery process. (Cal. Code Civil Proc. sec. 2023.010(d).) Petitioner failed to timely respond to Respondent's Specially Prepared Interrogatories (Set One) Nos. 2-10, 14-19, 28-30, 32 and 33, and is therefore subject to sanctions. Additionally, Petitioner failed to meet and confer in good faith insofar as she failed to respond to Respondent's multiple attempts to meet and confer. (Corey Declaration, Exhs. D, E, & F.).

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CARTE REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion To Compel Further Responses To Specially Prepared Interrogatories; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities (Added For Posting Of The Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: (part 2 of 2) Petitioner Gayle Draper-Brown and her counsel, Howard Garfield and Sasha D. Collins of Victorium Legal Inc., jointly and severally, are ordered to pay Respondent Lawrence W. Carte three thousand two hundred and ten dollars ($3,210.00), within 15 days of service of Notice of Entry of this Order. The prevailing party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DORIS TAYLOR
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO DETERMINE SUCCESSION TO REAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/19/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (Notice mailed before a pleading is e-filed - when it becomes publicly viewable - is defective. [LRSF 14.14 A.) (2) Do the grandchildren who disclaimed have issue? If so, those issue are now entitled to succeed. (3) Did Lenda Taylor die intestate? Is Isaiah (Isaiha or Isiaha?) Taylor her only successor-in-interest? (4) Clayton E. Flenoid has not disclaimed (or assigned) his interest. (5) Item 4 of the amended petition is incomplete. (6) Who was the predeceased spouse, and when did he die? (7) Item 11 of the amended petition indicates that there is also personal property at issue, but item 1 did not have the box checked for personal property. (8) Please also file a copy of the deed, showing the vesting of the real property. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 11/8/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (A revised proposed order is not an "Amended Order." Only an order that has been entered and then amended by a further Court order is an "amended order.")

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GREGORIO O. TAPIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Status hearing on close of estate will be set for 9/23/25. No appearance will be necessary on that date if either a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report of status of administration pursuant to Probate Code 12200 et seq is filed at least two weeks prior.
